text:
In a small, cozy town named Evergreen, there lived a quirky gardener named Lily. Her garden was unlike any other, filled with flowers of pastel colors that danced in the breeze. Every afternoon, she'd arrange them vividly in patterns for the townsfolk to enjoy during their picnics. However, Lily wasn't satisfied with just the ordinary floral designs. Her creations were imbued with stories—whispered secrets and dreams that blooms bore.

One chilly late autumn day, a sudden gust of wind sent petals flying. Among them was a peculiar pink flower with tiny, glowing eyes. Intrigued, Lily named it Ruby. Ruby had no leaves, and when the sun shone, it made a soft, light-hearted hum. Lily decided she had to know more about this enchanted flower.

She shared Ruby with her best friend, Otto the Owl, who was known far and wide for his wisdom. That night under the starlit sky, Otto explained that Ruby was the keeper
